description: This figure presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) inverse pole figure (IPF) maps and corresponding {100}, {110}, {111} pole figures illustrating the grain orientation and texture evolution of Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C low-density steel under varying cryogenic rolling (CR) deformation degrees. Subfigure (a) shows the undeformed solution-treated condition with equiaxed austenite grains (average size ~40 μm) exhibiting relatively random crystallographic orientations, weak texture intensity, and predominantly high-angle grain boundaries (94.65% HAGBs) including 60°<111> twin boundaries. Subfigure (b) displays the microstructure after 30% CR reduction, where grains elongate along the rolling direction (RD) and develop the copper {112}<111> texture component, with low-angle grain boundaries (LAGBs) significantly increasing to 89.4% and higher kernel average misorientation (KAM) values near prior grain boundaries. Subfigure (c) reveals a fully fragmented, fibrous banded structure after 90% CR, with austenite grains severely elongated along RD, LAGBs reaching 94.1%, and the texture transforming from copper to brass {110}<112> component, accompanied by distinct rolling bands and significantly increased local misorientation. This microstructural progression from equiaxed to elongated to fibrous morphologies with accompanying texture evolution directly correlates with the mechanical property trends of increasing strength and decreasing ductility reported in the study.
